title: rumex occidentalis var. procerus
intro: Rumex occidentalis var. procerus is a variety of the Western dock plant, a member of the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae), native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling, soothe wounds, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Rumex occidentalis var. procerus include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Rumex occidentalis var. procerus can be prepared as an herbal tea by steeping the leaves in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting its properties with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
